details: Soil Testing Lab - For analysing soil samples preceding the application of fertilizer in the correct dosage in farmers' field.
The analysis is free of cost of soil samples preceding application of fertilizers/ liming etc. for farmers along with distribution of Soil Health Card (SHC).
Bonafide farmers have sufficient available cultivable land on a first come first served basis.
benefits: To provide soil testing services for farmers free of cost.

offline: Bonafide farmers can apply in plain paper through the nearest Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/Agriculture Circle.

documents_required: Verification Form by the Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/Agriculture Circle.
Bank Details (Account No., IFSC Code) of the applicant.

faqs.question: What is Soil Testing Laboratory scheme ?
faqs.answer: For analysing soil samples preceding application of fertilizer in correct dosage in farmers field.

faqs.question: What is the benefits of this scheme ?
faqs.answer: To provide soil testing services for farmers free of cost and Distribution of Soil Health cards, Irrigation Water Quality testing.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for the scheme ?
How to avail the benefits of the scheme ?
faqs.answer: Bonafide farmers can apply in plain paper through the nearest Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/Agriculture Circle.

faqs.question: What is the mode of selection of Beneficiary ?
faqs.answer: Bonafide farmers have sufficient available cultivable land on a first come first served basis.

faqs.question: Is there any fee or charges for the scheme ?
Is there any age Restriction ?
faqs.answer: No, there is no age restriction.

faqs.question: Whom to contact regarding this scheme ?
faqs.answer: 1. District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts. 2. Sub-Divisional Agricultural Officers of the concerned sub-divisions. 3. District Training Officers, Tura/Shillong. 4. Agricultural Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks.

faqs.question: Is there any priority Female applicants ?
faqs.answer: No, its a first come first served basis.
